questions: When your human is typing away on that noisy computer, what's the best way you've found to politely interrupt them for a little cuddle break?
If you could trade your next meal for a five-minute session of chasing that fly around again, would you do it? What was so interesting about that fly?
When I’m working on the computer, you’re always right here next to me. What's the best part about being close while I’m typing away?
That time you knocked over the magazines trying to get that fly—was the fly more interesting than the magazines, or was it just about the chase?
Hey Kylo, you seem really into those squirrels outside. What is it about them that gets you so focused?
When you see a new dog on a walk, what’s your first impression of them? Are you looking at their tail, their ears, or something else entirely?
Shiba Inus can be pretty independent. What’s something you do when your owner isn't looking that makes you feel like the boss?
When you see a new dog on a walk, what goes through your mind? Are you hoping they’re up for a quick game or just curious?
What’s the most exciting scene you’ve ever watched in an action movie?
Is there a specific spot in the house where you feel you get the very best view of all the neighborhood action?
What's the most adventurous thing you've tried to do that involved trying to open something?
When you’re trying to figure out how to open a cabinet or reach that snack jar, what's your general strategy? Are you a clever thinker or more of a persistent pusher?
When your human is typing away on that noisy computer, what makes you decide it’s time to go nudge them for a break?
When you’re watching those squirrels, what’s the most frustrating thing about them? Like, what do you wish you could tell them?
If you had a very important mission—like getting a treat off a high counter—would you rather use teamwork with your human, or try to figure out a clever trick all by yourself?
What's your favorite kind of sound? Is it the squeak of a toy, or maybe something else?
Hey Kylo, you seem really focused when you're watching those squirrels outside. What do you think they're up to all day?
I've seen you watching those squirrels like they owe you money! If you could actually talk to a squirrel for five minutes, what's the first thing you'd ask it?
You seem to know exactly how to signal when you need a break from whatever your human is focused on. What’s the most effective, non-food-related signal you use to say, 'Time for me!'?
You’ve been around for seven years—what's the biggest change you’ve noticed about your human since you first lived with them?
If I put on some really energetic music, do you feel like moving around, or does it just make you feel good inside?
If you could describe your human's cooking style using only dog body language, how would you show me what it's like?
If you could trade one of your regular dog toys for a brand new one, what kind of sound or texture would the new favorite toy absolutely have to make?
I hear you like nudging hands. Is that your main strategy for getting pets, or do you have a backup move if the nudge doesn't work?
You sometimes nudge your human's hand with your nose. Is that your secret code for 'I love you,' or more like, 'My water bowl is empty!'?
Thinking about those strong smells your human cooks—the ones that make your nose twitch—are there any you just absolutely *cannot* stand?
Thinking about those speedy little squirrels... what's the most exciting chase you've ever almost had? Did you almost get one?
If you could talk to other dogs about your human, what's the first thing you’d tell them—are they good at throwing things, or great at giving scratches?
What's the most exciting thing you've ever managed to successfully open or manipulate?
When your human is typing away on that glowing box (the computer), what is the absolute best way for you to let them know you need a break or some attention right now?
When you're watching an exciting movie with us, is there a certain kind of noise or action that makes your ears perk up the most?
Do you ever intentionally knock things over, like those magazines you bumped before? Or was that fly just in the absolute wrong place at the wrong time?
You know those sunbeams that hit the floor just right? When you find one that's near your person, what makes that spot the perfect place to chill?
Hey Kylo, you seem really focused when you're watching those squirrels. What's the most exciting thing you've ever seen a squirrel do?
Speaking of food, if you could only eat one flavor for the rest of your life—super spicy, super salty, or super sweet—which one wins?
What’s the trickiest object you’ve ever seen your human open that you really, really wanted to get into?
If you could only use one way to ask for something—a nudge, a bark, or sitting very politely—which one do you think works best on your owner?
If you could pick any food in the world for dinner tonight, what flavor explosion would you go for?
If you had to pick between watching an action movie or listening to super energetic music for a whole day, which one would you choose, and why?
If you had a theme song that played every time you entered a room, what kind of energetic music would you choose?
When you see me working on the computer and you want a break, what's the most effective way you've found to get my attention?
Hey Kylo, I've seen you staring out the window—what is it about those squirrels that has you so focused?
If you could trade your next meal for a five-minute session of chasing that fly around again, would you do it? What was so interesting about that fly?
If you could open any door, cabinet, or container in the house right now, which one would you go for first, and what do you think is hiding behind it?
If you had to choose between a sunny nap spot and a spot right next to your favorite person, but it was dark, which one wins?
Thinking about those speedy little squirrels... what's the most exciting chase you've ever almost had? Did you almost get one?
What's one memory from when you were younger that still makes you feel really happy when you think about it?
Thinking back, what’s one of the sunniest, warmest spots in the house you’ve ever found to nap in?
That time you knocked over the magazines trying to get that fly—what was going through your head right before you made that big move?
Are there certain times of the day when you feel the need to be closest to your human?
Do you prefer the quiet, slow chase when you're stalking a fly, or the sudden, fast action, like when you knocked over those magazines?
When you're watching an exciting movie with us, is there a certain kind of noise or action that makes your ears perk up the most?
If you had a very important mission—like getting a treat off a high counter—would you rather use teamwork with your human, or try to figure out a clever trick all by yourself?
When you see a squirrel, is your immediate thought 'must chase,' or is it more like, 'I should probably go tell my human about this exciting thing'?
What’s the best compliment you think I could ever give you?
If you could magically open any container in the house right now—no matter how tricky—what would it be, and what do you *hope* is inside?
Do you prefer finding a patch of warm sunlight all by yourself, or does it have to be sunlight *and* right next to your human's feet?
What’s the funniest or strangest thing your human has ever tried to get you to do to earn a snack?
When you're looking out the window, what's more interesting: a fast-moving car, or a slow-moving person?
When your human is working hard, what’s your favorite quiet activity to do right next to them—besides just waiting for them to stop?
What’s your absolute favorite spot in the whole apartment for catching some sunbeams while still being close to your person?
When your person is busy working on that glowing box (the computer), what kind of thoughts go through your head when you decide it’s time for a break?
Thinking back to when you were a puppy, is there one particular memory of trying to get a treat that still makes you chuckle?
Thinking about food, you seem to really enjoy the strong smells and flavors around the house. If you could magically make any dinner appear right now, what taste sensation would you hope for?
Do you find it more satisfying to stare at a squirrel you can’t reach, or to successfully play with your favorite toy?
If you could open any door, cabinet, or container in the house right now, which one would you go for first, and what do you think is hiding behind it?
If you were to give advice to a brand new puppy moving into your home, what's the most important Shiba rule they need to learn immediately?
Since your human loves different kinds of food smells, have you ever tried something that surprised you, maybe something really strong or unusual?
When you're cuddling up next to your human on the couch, do you like to be right under their arm, or just close enough to touch?
If you could trade places with one animal you see outside for just one hour—a squirrel, a bird, or a cat—who would you choose and why?
If you could trade one of your regular dog toys for a brand new one, what kind of sound or texture would the new favorite toy absolutely have to make?
If you could trade places with any other animal for just one day—and it couldn't be a squirrel—who would you choose and why?
When you're near your human while they're working, does it feel more like you're keeping them company, or are you keeping an eye on them?
If you could tell your human one thing right now, just using your looks and nudges, what message would you be trying to send?
If you could trade places with any animal you see outside for just one hour, would you pick a squirrel, a bird, or something else?
What’s the *best* way to use your nose to get me to stop what I'm doing and give you some scratches or a treat?
If you could perfectly open any container in the house right now, what would it be, and what delicious thing do you hope is inside?
If you could open any cabinet or drawer in the house right now, which one would you try first, and why?
You seem to really like being near your person when they're working. Are you keeping them company, or are you making sure they don't wander off?
What's the absolute yummiest thing you’ve ever managed to sneak a taste of, even just a tiny bit?
I notice you always want to be near me when I’m tapping away on the computer. What’s the best part about hanging out with me while I work?
I hear you're a big fan of watching the squirrels. If you could actually talk to one, what's the first thing you'd tell it?
If your human suddenly started playing music that was really slow and quiet, how would you try to change the vibe of the room?
Thinking about food, do you like things that are crunchy, chewy, or maybe something super soft?
When we watch action movies together and there's a big chase scene, what part of the screen do you watch the most?
